summary: The share of children entering foster care who are placed with relatives has fallen almost every year since Fiscal 2022. ACS's own performance reports link the decline to rising family poverty in New York City and a growing number of relatives who say they are unable to take in kin, along with more teenagers — who are harder to place with relatives — entering foster care; the city lowered its own Fiscal 2026 target for this measure for the same reasons.

sources.label: ACS, Fiscal 2026 Preliminary Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: decreasing, which ACS links to rising family poverty and fewer available relatives
